evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: exceptionally clean, well-documented, semantically consistent, and structured with AI consumption in mind; LLM-based tools require to reason over data reliably; data quality monitoring and alerting
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities emphasize data quality, documentation, consistency, and reliability as foundational requirements—rigour signals dominate with no velocity language.
evidence.domain_risk: people data; headcount planning; attrition; absence; compensation
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Human resources and compensation data are moderately sensitive but not regulatory/compliance-driven; errors affect workforce decisions without legal/financial reporting consequences.
evidence.collaboration_width: people analysts; people business partners; finance; Data Engineering; People Tech; Internal Products
evidence.data_team_maturity: join an established team; data platform; analytics engineering function; data platform team; established semantic layer (Looker)
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Multiple named data sub-teams (analytics engineering, data platform) and 'join an established team' phrasing indicate mature, specialized organization.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build, expand, and maintain reusable data models and metrics in source of truth semantic layer (currently in dbt); Co-build and extend our people data plugin for AI tools such as Claude and Cursor; Implement data quality monitoring and alerting
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tools (dbt, Looker, Databricks, S3, Claude, Cursor), named AI integrations, and concrete responsibilities clearly written by technical hiring manager.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: People Analytics layer; people insights; data models and metrics for reporting and BI; self-service data exploration
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary focus is on internal data infrastructure, self-serve analytics, and enabling downstream analysts and stakeholders with trusted data sources.
evidence.autonomy_level: you will own; Own and evolve; Co-build and extend; establish standards and best practices; actively shape
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Multiple ownership and direction-setting verbs (own, establish, shape) demonstrate strategic role with authority over data infrastructure priorities.
evidence.ai_role: Co-build and extend our people data plugin for AI tools such as Claude and Cursor; structured with AI consumption in mind; LLM-ready documentation; conversational analytics
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Candidate is expected to build data infrastructure that AI systems (Claude, Cursor) consume, making this explicitly ai_enabler.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement data quality monitoring and alerting so issues are caught before they surface in dashboards or AI tools
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality and monitoring are framed as named ownership responsibilities, not just tool familiarity.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: exceptionally clean, well-documented, semantically consistent; data quality monitoring and alerting so issues are caught before they surface; ensure data integrity and efficiency; trustworthiness of data for AI
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High framing: repeated emphasis on data integrity, quality assurance, and trust as foundational concerns—fear is bad data reaching decision-makers and AI systems.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build, expand, and maintain; Set up and manage; Own and evolve
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of build/setup (greenfield) and maintain/expand (fix/scale)—role spans both infrastructure creation and ongoing evolution.
